title: PRED-106 — connect phantom WIN-071 citation to broader §2 self-contradiction pattern
target: data/predictions.json entry PRED-106 detail_reasoning final paragraph — currently the phantom WIN-071 finding is a standalone single-instance observation. Per ISS-1814, this should be connected to §2 (Self-Contradictions) as one instance of a broader internal-consistency pattern (the V13 self-referential loop in §2.9, hardcoded-as-derived numerical agreements, post-hoc parameter retuning) so that readers see it as evidence of a recurring drift, not a one-off typo.
summary_for_decider: Single targeted prose addition to PRED-106 detail_reasoning. Adds two sentences after the existing phantom-WIN-071 observation, framing it as the cleanest instance of the same internal-consistency drift pattern documented in §2 (specifically §2.9 V13 self-referential loop). No new arguments — the §2 pattern is already in the live site; this patch links PRED-106's specific finding to that pattern so the reader has the connective tissue. review_class=verification because the deep adversarial work (cataloguing the §2 pattern, identifying WIN-071 as phantom) is already in the live data; this patch is the linking sentence the reviewer asked for in ISS-1814.

patches.old_string: The phantom WIN-071 citation is independently flag-worthy: it suggests internal-consistency drift between the prediction registry and the wins registry.
patches.new_string: The phantom WIN-071 citation is independently flag-worthy: it suggests internal-consistency drift between the prediction registry and the wins registry. This is one instance of a broader pattern documented in §2 (Self-Contradictions) — the V13 Finsler coordinate system in §2.9 takes globe distances as input and outputs 'predicted' distances back to those same inputs (a closed loop, not a prediction); WIN-by-WIN numerical agreements often have parameters fitted retrospectively to the empirical target they are then claimed to confirm; and dome version notes (V51.0 → V51.1) repeatedly retune parameters across releases without preserving prior derivations. A prediction that cites a WIN that does not exist is the cleanest visible case of the same drift: the prediction's stated empirical basis is forward-referenced into a registry that hasn't caught up — which is exactly the failure mode you'd expect when prospective claims and confirming evidence are authored by the same agentic loop without an immutable separation between the two.
patches.rationale: ISS-1814 requested that the phantom-WIN-071 finding be connected to the broader §2 self-contradiction pattern rather than left as a single-instance observation. The new sentences (a) name three specific kinds of internal-consistency drift in §2 (V13 self-referential loop, retrospectively-fitted parameters, version-note retuning), (b) explicitly cross-reference §2.9 by name (the strongest §2 sub-section for this purpose), and (c) name the structural cause (no immutable separation between prediction registration and confirming evidence — which is also the methodology gap our Timestamp Error tab covers). This is the linking text the reviewer asked for; no new claims about the dome's model — only connective tissue between existing site sections.
patches.verification_class_justification: Prose patch adding ~140 words to a single field. No verdict change (PRED-106 stays at silent_ignore). No new dome-side claims fact-checked. No new sources cited beyond what is already in §2 of the live site. Cross-references §2.9 by name (a section that exists at the time of writing — verified). Review focus: confirm the prose lands without breaking sentence flow and that the §2.9 reference still resolves at integration time.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome model IS built with serious engineering attention to numerical agreement — the V13 Finsler formula in §2.9 is the most ambitious quantitative output in the entire site, and the WIN registry does score against published reference values. The internal-consistency drift we're flagging is not 'the author is sloppy' — it's a structural feature of any system where prospective claims and confirming evidence are authored by the same agentic loop without immutable separation. The phantom WIN-071 is a fingerprint of the methodology, not a typo.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Connecting the phantom citation to the §2 pattern strengthens our PRED-106 verdict (silent_ignore at window close), it doesn't save the dome's claim. The kernel here is methodological — the dome is doing serious work, but the methodology has a structural drift that surfaces visibly when a prediction cites a non-existent WIN.

anticipated_objections.objection: The new sentences include 'parameters fitted retrospectively to the empirical target they are then claimed to confirm' — that's a strong claim. Is it sourced?
anticipated_objections.response: Yes, in §2 itself: §2.4 (Solar Diameter variation) documents the n(r) refraction profile being fitted to recover the observed 52% diameter variation rather than derived from first principles; §2.6 (Polaris distance) documents 4,250 km being a fit-to-angle output, not a measured altitude; §2.9 (V13) is the explicit case the new sentence is built around. The new prose does not introduce these as new claims — it summarizes patterns the reviewer can verify by reading §2.

anticipated_objections.objection: The Timestamp Error tab reference at the end is implicit ('no immutable separation between prediction registration and confirming evidence') — is this too obscure for a reader?
anticipated_objections.response: It's intentionally implicit. The Timestamp Error tab makes the explicit case; this sentence here is structural framing ('this is what the failure mode looks like in the wild'). A reader who's already absorbed the Timestamp Error tab will recognize the framing; a reader who hasn't will still understand the structural cause from the sentence alone. Naming the tab inline would force a hyperlink that doesn't add information; the connection is already there for anyone who reads the site in section order.

anticipated_objections.objection: Is 'authored by the same agentic loop' too inside-baseball? A non-technical reader may not parse it.
anticipated_objections.response: Marginal. 'Agentic loop' is jargon-y but accurate. The surrounding clause 'without an immutable separation between the two' carries the structural point even if the reader skips 'agentic loop.' If curmudgeon flags this in a follow-up, an acceptable alt-text is 'authored by the same iterative-revision process' — same meaning, lower jargon density. We're not patching that now because the current text is honest about the dome's authorship method (Nick Hughes builds the dome agentically per CLAUDE.md / SESSION-CONTEXT.md).

notes_to_curmudgeon: Single mechanical string_replace in data/predictions.json [id=PRED-106].detail_reasoning. ~140 words added. No verdict change. The §2.9 cross-reference is a name-only mention (no anchor link) — if curmudgeon wants an anchor, recommend the section-id 'section-2-9' (verified present in data/sections.json id=part2). Combined diff: ~1 KB.
